summary: Refactored the large assembly file `asm/melee/lb/code_8001E2F8.s` without translating it to C: removed unused `.global` directives, renamed file-local `func_` symbols to `local_`, renamed data labels according to apparent type/content/usage, removed non-global declarations from broad headers, and added decomp.me/signature comments for matched functions. The PR was preparatory cleanup for a later split into named segments.

key_files.role: Main assembly refactor target; most changes rename local functions/data labels, remove unused globals, and update branch/call references.
Removed declarations for functions determined to be non-global or unused externally.
Removed declarations for variables determined to be non-global or unused externally.

what_changed: Renamed non-global function labels from `func_XXXXXXXX` to `local_XXXXXXXX`, including examples such as `func_8001E560` to `local_8001E560`, `func_8001E60C` to `local_8001E60C`, `func_8001EB14` to `local_8001EB14`, `func_8001EBF0` to `local_8001EBF0`, `func_8001ECF4` to `local_8001ECF4`, `func_8001EF5C` to `local_8001EF5C`, `func_8001F06C` to `local_8001F06C`, `func_8001F13C` to `local_8001F13C`, and `func_8001F294` to `local_8001F294`.

Updated internal branch/call sites to use the new `local_` names, for example `bl func_8001E560` became `bl local_8001E560`, `bl func_8001EB14` became `bl local_8001EB14`, and `bl func_8001F294` became `bl local_8001F294`.

Renamed anonymous data labels to more descriptive temporary names based on observed use, such as `lbl_803BADB0` to `str_arr_803BADB0`, `lbl_803BAE14` to `str_Warning_frame_offsets_not_supported`, `lbl_803BAE3C` to `str_arr_803BAE3C`, `lbl_804D3830` to `str_0`, `lbl_804333E0` to `skip1D8_804333E0`, and `lbl_804335B8` to `skipA0_804335B8`.

Preserved externally visible/global functions such as `func_8001F410`, `func_8001F578`, `func_8001F5C4`, `func_8001F5D4`, `func_8001F5E4`, `func_8001F5F4`, `func_8001F604`, `func_8001F614`, `func_8001F624`, `func_8001F67C`, `func_8001F800`, `func_8001F87C`, and `func_8001F890` while still updating their references to renamed data labels.
Removed 33 lines from `include/functions.h` and 67 lines from `include/variables.h`, matching the stated intent to keep non-global symbols out of shared headers.
Added decomp.me links and signature comments to matched functions, according to the PR body, but the provided diff excerpt does not show the exact comments.
smart_moves: Separated symbol-scope cleanup from C translation: the PR made the assembly file easier to split and decompile later without changing implementation logic.
Used `local_XXXXXXXX` as an explicit marker for functions that should not be declared in `functions.h`, reducing accidental external dependencies.
Kept signature information as comments instead of retaining unnecessary header declarations, because the author noted the signatures were either already defined elsewhere or unused.

Renamed data labels with conservative evidence-based placeholders such as `str_arr_`, specific string contents, and `skip<size>_address`, improving readability without overclaiming final semantic names.
Updated all assembly references consistently after label renames, which is essential for preserving assembly matching and avoiding unresolved labels.
decomp_lessons: Before splitting a large assembly file into named segments, first clean up symbol visibility: remove unused `.global`s and distinguish local labels from externally callable functions.

Do not keep non-global or unused symbols in monolithic headers just to document signatures; comments near the assembly can preserve useful decomp notes until C translation makes the declarations real.

Temporary label names can still encode useful information: `str_` or `str_arr_` for string data, string-content-derived names for unique messages, and `skip<size>_ADDR` for partially understood data blocks.
When renaming local functions in asm, update both the label definition and every `bl` target in the same file to keep the binary-equivalent assembly coherent.
Header cleanup is part of matching/decomp hygiene: broad `functions.h` and `variables.h` should not accumulate private implementation details.
Evidence is limited to the PR body and diff excerpt; there were no review comments in the provided context.
naming_conventions: `local_XXXXXXXX` denotes a non-global function at address `0xXXXXXXXX` that should not be exported in `functions.h`.
`func_XXXXXXXX` remains for functions that are still treated as global/exported or not yet semantically named.
`str_arr_XXXXXXXX` denotes data inferred to be an array or cluster of strings at address `0xXXXXXXXX`.
`str_<content>` can be used for identifiable string literals, as with `str_Warning_frame_offsets_not_supported`.
`str_0` was used for a small SDA string-like label replacing `lbl_804D3830`.
`skip<size>_XXXXXXXX` names partially understood data regions by a skipped byte size and address, as with `skip1D8_804333E0` and `skipA0_804335B8`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Remove `.global` directives for labels that are only referenced locally in the assembly file.
Rename file-local function entry labels to `local_` while preserving their address suffixes for traceability.
Retarget direct branch-and-link instructions after local renames, e.g. `bl func_8001F06C` to `bl local_8001F06C`.
Keep global labels for externally visible functions while allowing their internal references to renamed data symbols.
Use decomp.me links and nearby signature comments as lightweight documentation for matched functions before full C migration.
Prefer conservative data-label inference over speculative final names when the file is still assembly.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that split `asm/melee/lb/code_8001E2F8.s` into named segments.
Search for `local_8001EB14` or `local_8001ECF4` to identify the later C translation or semantic names.
Search for `skip1D8_804333E0` and `skipA0_804335B8` to see whether the placeholder data structures were later typed.
Search for `str_Warning_frame_offsets_not_supported` to locate the final owning subsystem or source file.
Compare later changes to `include/functions.h` and `include/variables.h` for continued removal of non-global symbols.
